surface.name: Studio Web template-guided first workflow run
surface.selection_basis: UiPath's Studio Web overview calls the browser-only product a great starting point, requires no installation, and directly recommends creating an automation from an available template. It says the template route provides a guided experience through triggers and activities, lets the user run a first workflow, and then publish it, making the successful first run the earliest documented functional terminal.

alternatives_considered.name: Studio Web project from scratch
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The overview recommends templates, while a blank project requires choosing a trigger and first activity without a canonical workflow outcome.

alternatives_considered.name: Studio Web project generated with Autopilot
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This requires authoring and refining a natural-language automation request rather than following the recommended template route.

alternatives_considered.name: Studio Desktop
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Studio Web is explicitly positioned as a browser-only starting point with no installation or IT dependencies.

entry_point.developer_intent: Start without a UiPath account or project, sign up for free, enter Studio Web, use its recommended template-guided experience, run the resulting workflow on the provisioned serverless robot, and verify that the run succeeded.
assumed_prior_state: No UiPath account, Automation Cloud organization, Studio Web role, department or service preferences, template selection, application connection, project, robot configuration, workflow run, or published automation is assumed.
A supported web browser, working identity, and network connection are available.
No signup data was submitted, external identity or application account was connected, project was created, automation was run, or artifact was published during research.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Run your first workflow from Studio Web's template-guided experience.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: A project created from a UiPath template runs on the default Automation Cloud Serverless robot and reaches a succeeded run status.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The Run output panel opens automatically, reports the run status as `succeeded`, and the successfully executed activities show green checkmarks in the designer.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The overview explicitly says the guided template experience lets the user run a first workflow, and the run documentation defines `succeeded`, but the live signup and Studio Web applications do not expose their current entry transitions publicly and the docs do not select one universal template.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The first successful workflow execution is earlier than publishing or operating an automation and is directly named by the getting-started overview. The terminal is appropriate, but the full no-account route cannot be asserted through the hidden application boundary and template-specific configuration.

prerequisites.requirement: Use a web browser; Studio Web requires no installation or operating-system-specific setup for its cloud route.
Have a UiPath account and Automation Cloud access, or use the linked free signup for commercial or non-commercial use.

Have a Studio Web Administrator, Studio Web Contributor, or Organization Template Author role that permits designing and running projects; default invited users normally receive Contributor through the Everyone group.
Have access to any external application accounts and connections required by the selected template.

primary_path.action: Open the Studio Web overview and follow its link to Studio Web or the free UiPath signup if no account exists.
details: The overview identifies Studio Web as a browser-only starting point.
It recommends templates and names running a first workflow as part of the guided experience.
primary_path.output: The live UiPath cloud or Studio Web application is reached.
primary_path.success_signal: The browser reaches the official application entry linked by the docs.

primary_path.action: Stop before entering or submitting identity information in the live UiPath cloud application.
details: The linked cloud application is client-rendered and does not provide publicly readable current signup fields or post-submit states.
The Studio Web application likewise requires live account context before its onboarding and template inventory can be observed.
primary_path.output: No account, organization, or Studio Web state is changed.
primary_path.success_signal: The last independently observable public boundary is the live, unsubmitted UiPath application.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The exact signup, authentication, organization, onboarding, and Studio Web convergence states are not established by public official content.

candidate_path_gap.reason: The client-rendered UiPath cloud and Studio Web applications do not publicly expose their current signup fields, authentication alternatives, validation, organization creation, tenant selection, or post-login transition. The documented continuation is to enter Studio Web; on first open choose a department and the services used so UiPath can recommend templates; open Templates; choose a template; inspect its details; supply any required template configuration and external application connections; select Use template; provide any still-required information or defer it into the project; open the created workflow in the designer; run Debug on cloud using the default provisioned Automation Cloud Serverless robot; and inspect the automatically opened Run output panel until its status is succeeded and executed activities show green checkmarks.
candidate_path_gap.impact: The intended first-run terminal is documented, but the no-account access sequence and the template-specific credentials, connections, fields, trigger, and visible outcome cannot be reconstructed without observing live private screens or selecting a template and external service for the developer.

branches.condition: The developer creates from a template
branches.path: Choose a template, provide its required configuration and connections, select Use template, and open the generated workflow.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Uses the route recommended by the getting-started overview, but required fields vary by template.

branches.condition: The selected template requires an external application
branches.path: Create or select each required account connection and configure any required source, destination, folder, or trigger details.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds external credentials, authorization, and service-specific configuration before the workflow can run.

branches.path: Open Workspace, select Create New and RPA Workflow, choose a manual, scheduled, or application-event trigger, name the project, and add a first activity.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces the template-guided route with open-ended workflow design and no universal output.

branches.path: Describe the automation in natural language, review and refine the generated preview, then select Continue to build the project.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds prompt authorship and preview judgment before execution.

branches.condition: The workflow runs in the cloud
branches.path: Use Debug on cloud with the account's selected robot type, which is Automation Cloud Serverless by default.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Requires no local UiPath Assistant installation.

branches.condition: The workflow runs on the local machine
branches.path: Install and run UiPath Assistant 2024.10.5 or newer and sign in to Assistant and Studio Web with the same user and organization.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds a desktop installation and identity-alignment requirement.

friction_gates.description: A UiPath account and Automation Cloud access are required to enter Studio Web.
The current cloud signup and application entry are client-rendered, so exact public form fields and transitions are not available.
Designing and running Studio Web projects requires one of the documented Studio Web roles.
First entry asks for department and services, and the developer must choose among multiple recommended templates.
Some templates require external application connections that may need to be created before use.
Template-specific required information must be supplied before creation or completed later inside the project.
Each cloud run builds the project, initializes the robot machine, and transfers the project and dependencies before workflow execution.

time_to_first_success.value: UiPath says users can get started in minutes and build automations without code; it does not scope this claim to the full no-account route through a succeeded run.

sources.title: Studio Web - Creating a project
sections_used: Creating a project from a template

sources.title: UiPath
evidence_supported: No publicly readable server-rendered account form or continuation states

sources.title: Studio Web - Managing access to Studio Web
Studio Web - Viewing the run output
evidence_supported: Running, succeeded, failed, paused, and stopped statuses

uncertainties.question: What exact identity, signup, organization, tenant, and authentication screens appear for a new UiPath user?
uncertainties.impact: The no-account entry path cannot be represented atomically.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official docs link directly to a client-rendered cloud application whose current form and transitions are not publicly readable.

uncertainties.question: Which template should define the universal first workflow and what exact output should it produce?
uncertainties.impact: Template choice changes required connections, fields, trigger, workflow activities, and value signal.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: UiPath recommends choosing an available template and personalizes recommendations from department and service selections, but does not select one universal template.

uncertainties.question: What exact duration applies from no account to a succeeded first workflow?
uncertainties.impact: A reliable time-to-first-success value cannot be stated.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The overview says users can get started in minutes but does not attach a measured duration to the full signup, template configuration, and first-run path.

excluded_after_success: Publishing the automation, deploying and scheduling it for ongoing use, sharing and collaboration, production governance, additional projects, local-machine execution, and operating published jobs occur after the first workflow run reports succeeded.
